items.title: Fix App/Universal Link Routing Issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves critical issues with App/Universal Link files and the routing of `/i/` endpoints. Key changes include serving verification files as static assets to prevent HTTP 423 errors and modifying the routing to ensure proper access to the Django API. These improvements will enhance the reliability of link handling in the application.

items.title: Enhance Invite Landing Accessibility in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes the invite landing at a new API endpoint, allowing it to be accessed via `/api/v1/attribution/landing/<id>`. The change ensures that the landing page is reachable in production by routing correctly through the Django backend. Additionally, the responses are configured with `Cache-Control: no-store` to prevent caching of per-click redirects, enhancing user experience.

items.title: Reduce Icon Size for Empty State in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: The central icon for the 'No historical tasks' empty state has been resized for better visual consistency across multiple service pages. The changes include reducing the icon size and adjusting the vertical padding in the shared NoTasks component. This update will enhance the user experience by providing a more balanced layout across approximately 20 service panels.

items.title: Fix event handling to prevent errors in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where `event.stopPropagation()` was called on potentially undefined events, leading to errors in various components. By implementing optional chaining, the code now safely handles missing events without throwing exceptions. This change enhances stability across multiple pages including veo, chatgpt conversations, and more.

items.title: Enhance auto-reload for stale chunks in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a robust auto-reload mechanism for handling stale dynamic-import failures after deployments. By porting the chunk load error handler from other frontends, users will now experience a seamless reload when encountering errors related to outdated chunk hashes. The changes ensure improved user experience and stability across the platform.

items.title: Enhance auto-reload for stale chunks in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a robust auto-reload mechanism for handling stale dynamic imports in the AuthFrontend application. By integrating a new chunk load error handler, the app will now automatically reload when it detects chunk-load errors, ensuring a smoother user experience post-deployment. The solution has been tested and is expected to significantly reduce errors related to outdated chunk references.

items.title: Enhance Docker Push Reliability in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the reliability of the Docker image push process by implementing a retry mechanism. If a ReadTimeoutError occurs, the system will retry the push up to five times with a 20-second backoff, preventing transient issues from blocking successful deployments. This change addresses failures seen in previous deploys.

items.title: Enhance Access Control with WeChat ID Matching in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature allowing access control to match users by either their WeChat ID or display name. The `is_allowed` and `get_trust_level` functions now accept a list of identifiers, enhancing flexibility while maintaining backward compatibility. Additionally, the `_handle` function has been updated to log both identifiers, ensuring better tracking and security.

items.title: Enhance Production Attribution Environment Setup in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the necessary `ATTRIBUTION_*` environment variables to the production deployment configuration. Key values for Apple and Android attribution have been specified, ensuring proper setup for the attribution endpoints. The changes will facilitate smoother operations and integration for the production environment.

items.title: Enhance Cookie Connector UX with Proactive Detection in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the cookie-connector connect dialog by adding proactive detection for the ACE extension and an install guide. Users will now see a banner prompting them to install the ACE extension if it's not detected, along with a link to the setup documentation and a re-check button. Additionally, internationalization support has been expanded with new keys for multiple languages.

items.title: Decouple Cookie Image for Smooth Deployments in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves deployment issues by decoupling the cookie keep-alive image from the main Docker build process. The Dockerfile now uses a standard Python 3.10 base, ensuring compatibility with necessary dependencies, while the cookie image is built as a non-blocking step in the deployment workflow. This change guarantees that app, connector, and icon deployments can proceed without interruption.

items.title: Enhance Deferred Deep Linking for iOS/Android in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces deferred deep linking for new installs on iOS and Android, ensuring that invite links correctly attribute referrals even when the app is freshly installed. Key changes include the implementation of an InstallReferrer plugin for Android and clipboard token handling for iOS, along with necessary adjustments to the app's manifest and entitlements. This enhancement improves user acquisition tracking and referral reward accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Deferred Deep Linking for Referrals in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a backend solution for deferred deep linking, allowing new app installs on iOS and Android to capture referral data. Key features include the `AttributionClick` model for tracking clicks, new API endpoints for resolving invites, and improved handling of click data across platforms. This implementation aims to eliminate reliance on third-party services, enhancing user acquisition strategies.

items.title: Introducing RUM Telemetry Search Tool in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a client-side telemetry query tool for Tencent RUM, complementing the existing server-side logs. The new tool allows querying of frontend errors reported by Nexior, AuthFrontend, and PlatformFrontend, making it easier to debug issues like JS errors and API failures. Key files include a Python script for querying and documentation for usage.

items.title: Enhance Cookie Connectors with ACE Extension in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability for cookie connectors to be directly connectable through the ACE browser extension, eliminating the need for manual cookie pasting. Users can now log in and capture their session seamlessly, with enhanced internationalization support across multiple languages. The changes also include a timeout feature for detecting the extension's presence and improved payload handling for cookie data.

items.title: Updated Connector Icons for Clarity in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the placeholder icons for new connectors with verified logos from their respective sites, ensuring all icons return a valid HTTP 200 status. The Google Drive icon remains unchanged. This enhancement improves the visual consistency and user experience within the application.

items.title: Fix cookie-refresh image build issue in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a missing build toolchain in the `Dockerfile.cookie-refresh`, which caused the cookie-refresh image to fail during deployment. By adding the necessary packages for building C extensions, the cronjob image can now be successfully built and applied after deployment. This ensures that the connector sync continues to function smoothly.

items.title: Enhanced Telemetry Integration in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces Tencent RUM (Aegis) to the PlatformFrontend, completing the telemetry coverage across our frontend applications. Key changes include the addition of a new telemetry plugin that captures JS errors, API failures, and page performance metrics, ensuring better observability. The integration is designed to be non-blocking and resilient, enhancing the overall reliability of the developer portal.

items.title: Enhance Cookie Keep-Alive Functionality in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated cronjob for cookie keep-alive functionality within the AuthBackend, ensuring that connector sessions remain active. Key additions include a new Dockerfile for a browser-enabled image, management commands for refreshing cookie connections, and comprehensive testing for the new logic. This enhancement complements existing OAuth-token refresh mechanisms, improving overall connection management.

items.title: Fix catalog sync command for live connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The `sync_connector_catalog` command was renamed to `sync_connectors`, causing the catalog sync CronJob to fail and preventing new connectors from being synced to production. This update modifies the CronJob to use the correct command, ensuring that all seeded connectors are properly upserted on the next deployment. This fix will restore the live directory to include all recent connector additions.

items.title: Resolve App Store Rejection Issues in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses two critical issues that led to the rejection of Nexior by the App Store. It adds necessary camera and photo library usage descriptions to the Info.plist to prevent crashes when using the 'Take Photo' feature, and it ensures that the entire paid catalog is hidden on iOS to comply with Apple's guidelines. These changes enhance app stability and compliance with App Store requirements.

items.title: Enhance Security with Code-Enforced Trust in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the security model by enforcing trust levels directly in the code, eliminating reliance on prompts. Key changes include the removal of a hardcoded vendor denylist and the introduction of a more robust access control mechanism. The bridge now differentiates between admin and normal users, ensuring that only authorized users can access tools, enhancing overall system security.

items.title: Enhance Apple Login with Tencent RUM in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates Tencent RUM (Aegis) into the AuthFrontend, addressing the lack of telemetry for the Apple Sign-In process. Key changes include the addition of a new telemetry plugin, capturing Apple login events, and enhancing error handling across the authentication flow. This ensures that failures are now observable, improving overall user experience and debugging capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Apple Login with Timeout and Telemetry in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses silent hangs during Apple Sign-In by implementing a 20-second timeout for login requests. Key changes include adding timeouts to the HTTP client and the AuthPanel component, ensuring that stalled logins fail visibly and are tracked in telemetry. This improvement enhances user experience by preventing indefinite loading states.

items.title: Boost Gradle Heap to Fix OOM in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update increases the Gradle heap size from 1536m to 4096m to resolve OutOfMemoryError issues during the lintVitalRelease task. The change addresses failures in the Android build caused by the addition of new dependencies, ensuring smoother builds moving forward. The updated settings will be monitored in the next auto-triggered build.

items.title: Optimize face-swap probe interval for efficiency in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The continuous face-swap probe interval has been increased from 180 seconds to 600 seconds. This change reduces the call volume to the face-swap service by approximately two-thirds, while still ensuring timely detection of outages. The adjustment is made in the e2e test script without requiring any additional scheduling changes.

items.title: Reverting cookie keep-alive to AuthBackend in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update reverts the cookie keep-alive functionality back to the AuthBackend as per architectural decisions. The changes include the removal of related code from the Publisher, ensuring that the keep-alive process is handled by a dedicated cronjob instead. The publish-time cookie read functionality remains intact, maintaining overall system integrity.

items.title: Enable Push Notifications for iOS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the necessary entitlement for push notifications in the iOS app by declaring the `aps-environment` as production. The existing App ID already supports push notifications, ensuring a seamless integration without any changes to the signing process. Users can now receive Coding Bridge consent prompts via APNs after installing the app from TestFlight.

items.title: Enhance Connector Cookie Management in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a keep-alive mechanism for connector cookies, ensuring users remain logged in without needing to reconnect during session rotations. Key modifications include enhancements to the connections and scheduler modules, as well as the addition of eight new client tests to ensure functionality. The changes are designed to run seamlessly alongside existing refresh processes, improving overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance iOS Push Notifications with Direct APNs Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces direct APNs transport for iOS consent push notifications, allowing the system to send notifications directly via Apple's APNs HTTP/2 instead of relying on FCM. Key changes include new methods in notifier.py for APNs handling, updated configuration options, and modifications to the main API to support the new push kind. The implementation is fully tested, ensuring reliable delivery of notifications to iOS devices.

items.title: Enhance iOS Push Notification Support in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for registering native push notifications on iOS using APNs, while maintaining FCM for Android. Key changes include the addition of a new transport for APNs and updates to the subscription handling to differentiate between iOS and Android. The implementation is currently a no-op until the necessary iOS entitlements and configurations are completed.

items.title: Enhance Notification Bell Loading State in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update improves the loading state of the notification bell by replacing the cramped built-in spinner with a visually consistent Font Awesome spinner icon. The button is now disabled while busy, ensuring a cleaner user experience. Additionally, the Font Awesome spinner has been registered in the central plugin for better integration.

items.title: Enable Firebase Push Notifications for Android in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) into the Android app for Coding Bridge consent prompts. It adds the necessary `google-services.json` file, enabling push notifications when the app is backgrounded or closed. The server-side relay is already live, ensuring that consent prompts can be pushed effectively to users.

items.title: Revamp Empty-State Button for New Tasks in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: The empty-state magic wand has been transformed into a clickable button that opens the new task drawer, enhancing user experience. The previous floating magic button is now hidden during the empty state, providing a clearer interface. This change streamlines task creation on mobile devices, ensuring users have a single, intuitive option to start new tasks.

items.title: Stop iOS Auto-Zoom in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update resolves the issue of iOS auto-zooming the page when focusing on the coding-bridge composer. By increasing the font size of the textarea to 16px on mobile devices, we ensure a smoother user experience without affecting the desktop layout. The fix mirrors a previous solution applied to the chat composer.

items.title: Enhance cookie connectors with new platforms in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update expands the cookie-connector pilot by adding support for four additional Chinese platforms: CSDN, Juejin, Toutiao, and Xiaohongshu. Each connector is designed with a `credential_schema` for cookies, allowing users to securely capture and store login states via the ACE extension. Future phases will focus on publisher integration and cookie keep-alive functionality.

items.title: Enhance cookie handling from AuthBackend in Publisher 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates cookie connectors from the ACE extension into the Publisher, allowing for seamless access to cookies stored in AuthBackend's encrypted vault during the publishing process. Key changes include the addition of a new function to fetch connector cookies and modifications to the scheduler to prioritize these cookies over legacy credentials. The implementation is supported by new unit tests to ensure reliability.

items.title: Secure Admin Tabs for Mobile Users in Nexior 🔒
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where non-admin users could access admin-only settings on mobile devices. By rendering only visible navigation items, we ensure that sensitive tabs are not included in the DOM, effectively preventing unauthorized access. Additionally, a missing guard for the Site Settings tab has been added to enhance security.

items.title: Enhance Consent Notifications for Coding Bridge in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces out-of-band consent notifications for Coding Bridge when no browser tab is visible. It implements optional Web Push and FCM transports to ensure users receive prompts even when they are not actively engaged. Key changes include a new presence tracking system and a dedicated Mongo collection for push subscriptions, enhancing user experience and engagement.

items.title: User Notifications for Session Approval in Nexior 🔔
items.summary: This update introduces a notification system for user session approvals in Coding Bridge. Users will now receive alerts via background tabs, closed tabs, and mobile devices, ensuring they never miss important prompts. The implementation includes a new notification toggle and support for multiple notification types, enhancing user experience and engagement.

items.title: Enhance Permission Handling for Consistent Notifications in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a robust system for managing pending permission requests, ensuring they persist even when users are temporarily disconnected. Key changes include the implementation of a `PermissionBroker` that retains request descriptors and a new protocol for re-fetching outstanding prompts. This foundational work sets the stage for future enhancements in remote consent notifications.

items.title: Enhance Cookie Connectors for Chinese Platforms in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces cookie-only connectors for Chinese platforms, enabling seamless integration without the need for OAuth or API publishing. Key changes include the addition of a new `cookies` field type in the `validate_byoc_payload` function, allowing for the secure handling of browser cookie jars. The implementation also includes initial seeds for platforms like Zhihu, Weibo, and Bilibili, setting the stage for future expansions.

items.title: Enhance SEO with Canonical Redirects for Document Slugs in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a canonical redirect for old document slugs to their new aliases, addressing SEO concerns related to duplicate content. The changes ensure that when users access old URLs, they are seamlessly redirected to the canonical alias, improving search engine indexing. Additionally, the canonical link now correctly points to the alias instead of the UUID, further consolidating URL authority.

items.title: Fix onboarding key alignment for seamless integration in Open Claw Provider 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical mismatch between the onboarding option key and the CLI flag for non-interactive onboarding. The `optionKey` has been corrected to `acedataApiKey`, ensuring that user-provided API keys are properly stored and utilized. Additionally, a regression test has been added to prevent future discrepancies, and the version has been bumped to 2026.5.34.

items.title: Revamp MCP Documentation with New Naming Conventions in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a significant overhaul of 90 MCP documentation articles, renaming them to a cleaner format while ensuring old URLs remain functional through redirects. Additionally, 9 redundant articles have been removed, streamlining the documentation for better accessibility and clarity. The changes enhance user experience across all locales by updating 4242 inbound links.

items.title: Streamlined PyPI Publishing with Org Token in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update transitions the publishing process to utilize the organization-level `PYPI_TOKEN`, allowing for seamless publishing to PyPI upon merging to the main branch. The versioning follows a date-based format with a daily build number, ensuring that repeated merges do not conflict. Additionally, the workflow supports manual triggers and includes a safeguard for re-runs with the `--skip-existing` option.

items.title: Enhance Publisher Connectors with OAuth Support in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update transforms PlatformPublisher's manual credential handling into robust connectors for various platforms. Batch 1 introduces Dev.to and Blogger with BYOC and OAuth capabilities, while Batch 2 adds new OAuth2 adapters for Reddit and LinkedIn. Users can now seamlessly publish content from Studio chat, enhancing the overall publishing experience.

items.title: Exciting New Publisher Connectors for Skills in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new publisher connectors for platforms including Dev.to, Blogger, Reddit, and LinkedIn. Each connector allows users to publish and manage content seamlessly using their respective APIs. Additionally, a comprehensive guide has been added to assist users in setting up these connectors effectively.

items.title: Enhance Claude Integration and WeChat Login Experience in Frontend Web 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a streamlined Claude integration flow directly within the console, providing users with a copy-paste code snippet for easy setup. Additionally, it highlights the need for re-scanning the WeChat QR code after instance termination or redeployment. The changes include internationalization support across 12 locales, ensuring a broader accessibility for users.

items.title: Enhance PyPI Publishing Workflow in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the CI workflow to trigger automatic publishing to PyPI upon merging to the main branch, addressing previous issues with manual dispatch and tag-based triggers. Key changes include a unique CalVer timestamp versioning system and concurrency management to ensure safe re-runs. A one-time setup for PyPI's pending publisher is required for trusted publishing, streamlining future releases.

items.title: Rearrange x402 Navigation Entry in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The x402 navigation entry has been successfully moved to the last position in the top navigation bar, following the ACE Token link. This change involved only the repositioning of a single menu item, with no internationalization modifications required. All relevant checks for eslint, prettier, and i18n have passed.

items.title: Upgrade WeChat Bot Image to Verified Snapshot in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates the WeChat Bot deployment image from `img-eyraq5nz` to the verified `img-7fdrlev9`, ensuring enhanced reliability. The change applies to both the public `wechat-bot` service and the legacy `wisdom` service, with no impact on existing deployments. The migration is idempotent and reversible, allowing for a seamless transition to the new image.

items.title: Publish-Ready WisdomChannel with PyPI & CLI Enhancements in Wisdom Channel 🚀
items.summary: This update makes WisdomChannel installable directly from PyPI and corrects the integration documentation. Key changes include a single version source derived from package metadata, improved CLI commands, and the addition of smoke tests to ensure functionality. The README now emphasizes the need for a persistent interactive TTY session for optimal operation.

items.title: Enforce Beachball Change File Check in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a critical change to the CI workflow by enforcing a beachball change file check for PRs. If a PR modifies publishable files without a corresponding change file, the CI will fail, preventing merges that would skip version bumps and mobile auto-releases. Additionally, a patch change file is included to ensure the full release process is triggered upon merging this PR.

items.title: Updated WeChat Bot Architecture Details in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The WeChat bot documentation has been corrected to accurately reflect the architecture, replacing the previous erroneous details with the correct Tencent CVM Windows host setup. Key updates include the introduction of the Wisdom API on port 8000, QR code login, and a revised cURL command for message sending. This change enhances clarity for users and aligns the documentation with the actual implementation.

items.title: Introducing YouTube Connector for Self-Serve Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a YouTube connector to the platform, allowing users to easily connect their YouTube accounts with a single click. The integration utilizes OAuth for authentication, storing refresh tokens securely, and supports both uploading and reading from YouTube. Users can now automate video uploads generated from the platform, enhancing their content publishing capabilities.

items.title: Empower YouTube Interactions with New Skill in Skills 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces a new YouTube skill that enables users to search YouTube, access their channel and uploaded videos, and upload new content through the YouTube Data API v3. The skill is automatically linked to the Google YouTube connector, enhancing user experience by allowing seamless interactions with their YouTube account. Key functionalities include video stats retrieval, comments access, and resumable uploads with user confirmation.

items.title: Enhance Document Aliases for Seamless URL Renaming in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature allowing documents to be renamed without breaking existing URLs. The `redirect_from` field now holds old slugs, ensuring that both new and old URLs resolve to the same document. This change enhances SEO by preventing 404 errors and maintaining inbound link integrity.

items.title: Enhance WeChat QR-login Experience in Frontend Web 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a WeChat QR-login card and a dispatcher alias for the `wechat-bot` service, enhancing the user experience in the console. Users can now log in seamlessly without RDP, as the QR code opens in a new tab for easy scanning. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for multiple languages, ensuring accessibility for a broader audience.

items.title: Unify WeChat Bot Service Identities in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update merges the two WeChat Bot service identities into a single public deployable service. The migration copies deployment metadata, cost, and packages from the private 'wisdom' identity to the public 'wechat-bot', ensuring users can access the deploy wizard seamlessly. Additionally, it adds a JSON file for consistent pricing across services.

items.title: Enhance Mobile Experience with AskUserQuestion Fix in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses mobile usability issues with the AskUserQuestion card in the coding bridge. Key changes include docking the question card above the composer to ensure the action bar remains visible and implementing Enter-to-advance functionality for radio selections. These improvements enhance user interaction and accessibility on mobile devices.

items.title: Enhance Markdown Rendering with Links and Syntax Highlighting in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the rendering of markdown links and syntax highlighting in the coding-bridge transcript. Key changes include enabling linkification for bare URLs and integrating highlight.js for reliable syntax highlighting directly in the rendered HTML. These enhancements ensure that links are clickable and code blocks are visually appealing, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Fix MCP Channel Card Token Key for Better Deployment Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves an issue with the MCP Channel card on the deployment management page, ensuring it correctly reads the API token after migration. The token is now fetched data-driven from deployment metadata, with fallbacks for legacy instances. This change enhances the user experience by restoring the intended functionality for both new and old instances.

items.title: Automate Mobile Testing Releases in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automated workflow for releasing mobile versions to testing tracks upon merging to the main branch. Android builds will go to Open Testing while iOS versions will be uploaded to TestFlight without submitting for App Store review. The process ensures that production releases remain manual, preventing any unintended promotions.

items.title: Enhance mobile usability of AskUserQuestion card in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the AskUserQuestion card was unusable on mobile devices due to clipping of the question text and buttons. By introducing a 'bounded' prop, the card now adapts its height appropriately within the transcript's scroll container, ensuring all options and the Submit button are accessible. Testing confirms that the chat functionality remains unchanged while improving the mobile experience.

items.title: Streamlined Document Links and Cleanup in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update implements UUID to alias link normalization across all 18 locales, ensuring all document links are now canonical. Additionally, 92 junk documents have been removed, including deprecated stubs and unpublished duplicates, enhancing the overall quality and accessibility of the documentation. The changes improve link consistency and reduce clutter in the documentation repository.

items.title: Updated API Guides with Latest Model Changes in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the aichat, glm, and flux API guides with the latest model specifications. Key changes include the removal of outdated models like `glm-4.5-air` and `flux-pro-1.1`, and the addition of `deepseek-v4-flash` and `flux-2-pro`. These adjustments ensure that the documentation reflects the current capabilities and options available to users.

items.title: Update aichat models with deepseek-v4-flash in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `deepseek-v4-flash` model to the aichat models, addressing a sync issue that has persisted since June 2026. Additionally, lint fixes were applied to ensure that the aichat CI pipeline runs smoothly again. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the integrity of the changes.

items.title: Sync aichat and glm model enums with Docs updates in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update manually reconciles the aichat and glm model enums with the latest OpenAPI documentation, addressing a sync failure that has persisted since June 2026. Key changes include the addition of new models and the removal of outdated ones from both aichat and glm. The PR also refreshes related documentation to reflect these updates.

items.title: Enhance x402 Discovery to Filter Payable Products in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new utility for filtering the x402 discovery document to ensure only payable products are advertised. Key changes include the addition of a pure function for path filtering and updates to the view to implement these rules, resulting in a reduction of discoverable endpoints from 72 to approximately 46. The changes improve the quality of the indexed services by excluding free and internal endpoints.

items.title: Enhance first turn experience with persistent thinking indicator in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes the issue where the 'thinking' animation disappears too quickly during the first turn of a session, making it appear frozen. By keeping the session status as 'running' on the 'session.started' event, the indicator remains visible until the turn is complete, improving user experience. Additionally, this change restores the interrupt button functionality during the first turn.

items.title: Enhance Video Product Billing Rules in Backend 🎥
items.summary: This update introduces new billing rules for the PlatformStudio video product, detailing costs for FFmpeg and Remotion engines. It includes 6 cost tests to ensure accuracy, which will run in CI and during Docker builds, thus securing the deployment process. The API billing rules and tests are now fully integrated, enhancing the overall functionality.

items.title: Enhance Agent Frameworks with X402 Payment Integration in X402 Client 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces several examples demonstrating how to integrate AceData Cloud APIs with agent frameworks using the x402 payment protocol. Key additions include tools for LangChain, OpenAI Agents, CrewAI, and an MCP server, all allowing agents to pay per call in USDC without needing API keys. The examples are designed for testing and showcase a streamlined approach to agent payments.

items.title: Fix broken link in directory submission docs in Index 🚀
items.summary: Updated the documentation for directory submission by replacing a dead link with the correct one. The previous link to the quickstart guide was returning a 404 error, and it has now been verified to return a 200 status. This change enhances the accessibility of the documentation for users.

items.title: Fix broken links and enhance resources in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a broken documentation link by redirecting it to the correct resource. Additionally, a new Resources section has been added, featuring eight verified links to essential tools and guides. The top navigation has also been updated to include an 'x402' menu item, improving accessibility across multiple languages.

items.title: Link Cleanup and Normalization Across All Locales in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update includes a comprehensive audit and correction of all links in the documentation for the platform, eliminating 37 distinct fake links across 18 locales. Key changes involve fixing URL typos, updating UUIDs to aliases, and ensuring all links point to valid resources, enhancing the overall user experience and accessibility of the documentation.

items.title: Enhance single-instance locking to prevent daemon conflicts in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a single-instance lock mechanism to prevent multiple daemons from running simultaneously and conflicting over a node token. The new implementation ensures that if a second instance is attempted to be started, it fails fast with a clear error message, enhancing reliability. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate the locking functionality, ensuring smooth operation under various scenarios.

items.title: Launch Checklist for PlatformStudio in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive deploy and go-live runbook for PlatformStudio. It includes essential steps such as setting up secrets, registering services, and performing an initial smoke test to ensure everything is functioning correctly. This checklist aims to streamline the deployment process and enhance operational efficiency.

items.title: Enhance Worker Path with Django Adapter and Publishing Features in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update completes the worker's production path, facilitating the Studio to Publisher data handoff. Key additions include the `DjangoPersistence` adapter, which implements the worker's persistence protocol, and new models for `PublishChannel`, `PublishDocument`, and `PublishTask`. The scheduler now effectively polls for pending video projects, ensuring a seamless workflow from production to publishing.

items.title: Fix Docker Build for Poetry Install in Platform Studio 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a failure in the Deploy workflow's Docker build due to Debian bookworm's handling of the system interpreter as externally-managed (PEP 668). By setting `PIP_BREAK_SYSTEM_PACKAGES=1`, poetry can now pip-install dependencies directly into the system interpreter. This change ensures smoother dependency management and builds.

items.title: Feat(automation+ci): trend selection + Docker→GHCR build + cron in Platform Studio ✨
Feat(api+deploy): project routes + K8s manifests in Platform Studio ✨
Docs: unify API application sections to universal-balance flow + source backlink (202 files) in Backend 📝
Feat(worker): end-to-end project processor + env-backed adapters in Platform Studio ✨

Introducing Grok Imagine Video Tool Plugin in Dify 🎥
items.summary: This update adds the Grok Imagine Videos plugin to Dify, enabling users to generate videos from text prompts and images. Key features include tools for video generation and task retrieval, enhancing the platform's multimedia capabilities. The plugin mirrors existing video tools, ensuring a consistent user experience.

items.title: Fix video storage path for Grok in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the storage path for Grok Imagine videos, ensuring they are saved under the `/grok` CDN path instead of the previously hardcoded `/sora`. Key changes include modifying the `transferVideos` and `uploadSoraVideosToCos` functions to utilize the new `DEFAULT_GROK_VIDEO_CDN_PATH`. The default behavior remains unchanged for other video types, maintaining consistency across the platform.

items.title: Introducing GrokMCP Server for AI Video Generation in M C Ps 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces the GrokMCP server, enabling AI video generation through the Grok Imagine service. Key features include tools for text-to-video and image-to-video generation, along with task tracking capabilities. The implementation is designed to enhance the existing MCP layer, providing a robust solution for video generation tasks.

items.title: Enhance Event Delivery Design and Protocol v2 in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces a comprehensive design document and protocol v2 specification aimed at improving event delivery reliability across three repositories. Key features include a per-session event-sourced log, a node-side outbox for buffering, and cursor-based resume capabilities. These enhancements are designed to ensure that in-flight events are not lost during disconnections, significantly improving the user experience.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Plugin Type Checking in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves type checking issues for the OpenClaw WeChat channel plugin by remapping imports to the new public API structure of the `openclaw` SDK. Additionally, it introduces essential build tooling, including a `tsconfig.json` and a type checking script, which previously did not exist. The changes ensure that the plugin is now compatible with `openclaw@2026.6.6`, significantly reducing type errors and improving development workflows.

items.title: Revamp Prompt Editing to Rewind Conversations in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update replaces the previous append behavior for prompt editing with a rewind/fork mechanism, ensuring that the conversation truncates correctly at the edited prompt. Key changes include the introduction of a new `session.edit` action and enhancements to the user interface, allowing users to edit prompts while maintaining session integrity. The implementation is paired with backend support for seamless session management.

items.title: Enhance Prompt Editing with Conversation Forking in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the ability to edit past prompts by forking the conversation, aligning with Claude's rewind functionality. Key changes include the implementation of a new `session.edit` action, support for editing capabilities in the Claude provider, and the addition of comprehensive unit tests. The changes enhance user experience by allowing more precise control over conversation history.

items.title: Enhance Try-the-API with Service Favicons in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces service favicons to the **Try the API** dropdown, enhancing the user interface. The `ApiKeyQuickAccess.vue` component now includes an `icon_url` for each service, while `ApiCodeDialog.vue` renders these icons alongside service names. This improvement follows previous updates to the applications list and leverages backend data already in place.

items.title: Remove SolRadar from Index tracking in Index 🚀
items.summary: The `SolRadar` submodule has been deregistered from the Index superproject, aligning it with other standalone workspace repositories. This change ensures that SolRadar remains a self-contained repo while no longer being tracked as a submodule. All working files are untouched, and its history and remote connection are preserved.

items.title: Fix favicon rendering issue by registering ElImage in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where the favicon `<el-image>` was not rendering due to the component not being registered. The `ElImage` component has now been imported and registered in the List.vue file, ensuring that the icons will display correctly. With backend data already in place, this change allows the icons for 42 API services to be visible.

items.title: Add animated thinking indicator for agent activity in Nexior 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces a new thinking indicator in the coding-bridge session view, enhancing user experience by providing visual feedback while the agent processes prompts. The indicator features a Braille spinner and playful gerunds, along with a live elapsed timer. This ensures users are aware that the agent is actively working, reducing confusion during wait times.

items.title: Update PR Workflow to Align with Backend in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The PR workflow for the frontend has been renamed from 'CI' to 'check-pr' to match the backend's naming convention. This change ensures that the required checks are accurately reported, preventing issues with force-merging PRs. A companion ruleset update will further align the frontend checks with the backend's requirements.

items.title: Fix replay issue for resumed transcripts in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue where new queries appeared mid-transcript after resuming a conversation. The fix involves guarding against replayed messages by filtering out old IDs, ensuring that only genuine new outputs are processed. Comprehensive tests confirm the solution's effectiveness and prevent regressions.

items.title: Exciting New Codex Slide Added to Homepage Carousel in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Codex hero slide to the homepage carousel, positioned right after the Claude Code slide. Key changes include the addition of a `slide--codex` item in the `Index.vue` file and updates to multiple language service files to support the new content. This enhancement aims to promote the new Codex Agent service effectively.

items.title: Introducing Codex Agent Service in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Codex Agent service to the platform, mirroring the existing Claude Code service. It features a bilingual landing page and integrates seamlessly with the existing OpenAI Responses-compatible proxy. No new API or billing is required, enhancing accessibility for users.

items.title: Edit and Resend Prompts in Nexior ✏️
items.summary: Users can now click on sent prompts in the Coding Bridge session view to edit and resend them. This feature allows for model switching and tweaking of text and attachments before resending, enhancing user interaction. The changes include updates to the session and transcript components, as well as internationalization support for multiple languages.

items.title: Enhance ID Visibility on Coding Bridge in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the display of diagnostic identifiers on the Coding Bridge page by replacing the truncation style with a wrapping style. Users can now view and copy the full Device/Node ID, Session ID, and Trace ID without any cutoff. The tooltip and copy-to-clipboard functionality remain intact for user convenience.

items.title: Add favicon support for API services in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a new `icon_url` field to the Service model, allowing each API service to display a favicon alongside its name in the console. A backfill script has been implemented to populate this field for existing services using their corresponding Document icons. The migration and backfill processes are set up to ensure a smooth rollout without external fetching.

items.title: Enhance Application List with Service Favicons in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces service favicons to the Dedicated Applications list, enhancing visual identification. The `IService` model now includes an optional `icon_url`, and the application list displays these icons next to service names when available. This feature improves the overall user experience by aligning the console with the document page design.

items.title: Enhance Composer Experience by Remembering Last Setup in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a feature that remembers the last composer setup used per device in the Coding Bridge. Key settings such as working directory, provider, model, reasoning effort, and permission mode are now persisted, allowing for a smoother user experience during new sessions. The implementation ensures that existing sessions remain unaffected while enhancing usability across devices.

items.title: Fix default action for task retrieval in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a bug where missing action parameters in `/tasks` queries resulted in empty responses. The default action is now set to 'retrieve', ensuring that clients can successfully poll tasks by ID without specifying the action. This change impacts multiple workers, enhancing the overall reliability of task retrieval across the platform.

items.title: Enhance Error Handling and Resilience in Coding Bridge Agent 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces structured error handling and self-healing capabilities to the Coding Bridge Agent. Key changes include improved logging for better observability, a reset mechanism for dead subprocesses, and a bounded retry feature for transient errors. These enhancements aim to provide a more robust and user-friendly experience during sessions.

items.title: Enhance Coding Bridge with Retry Button in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a **Retry** button in the Coding Bridge session view, allowing users to easily re-run the last prompt after an error occurs. The feature addresses transient crashes by enabling a quick retry without the need to retype the prompt. Additionally, internationalization support has been added for multiple languages.

items.title: Fix default action for tasks in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the default action for the `/<provider>/tasks` OpenAPI specifications, setting it to `retrieve` across all relevant specs. It also removes the requirement for the `action` parameter where a default is provided, streamlining the API usage. These changes enhance the clarity and functionality of the API documentation and SDK code generation.

items.title: Update ttapi Base URL for Improved TLS Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates the ttapi worker's base URL from `https://api.ttapi.org` to `https://api.ttapi.io` to resolve TLS verification issues. The previous URL caused significant 5xx errors due to an incomplete certificate chain, impacting user traffic. The new URL ensures a complete and valid TLS chain, enhancing reliability for ttapi-backed services.

items.title: Set Default Balance to General in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: The default selection for the balance card on the API docs page has been changed from 'Instance Balance' to 'General Balance'. This adjustment reflects user feedback, as the General Balance is the shared pool most API calls utilize. The implementation involved modifying the `selectedScope` in the `Remain.vue` component to default to 'global'.

items.title: Prettier formatting applied to multiple components in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update implements Prettier formatting across six files, ensuring consistent code style without any behavioral changes. Key adjustments include wrapping long lines, collapsing attributes, and standardizing multi-line types. The reformatting aligns these components with the project's Prettier configuration.

items.title: Enhance Mobile Experience with Scrollable Options in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the mobile user experience in the AskUserQuestion feature by ensuring that the Submit button remains visible and accessible. The option list now scrolls internally within a height-bounded card, preventing content clipping and ensuring all options are reachable. This change enhances usability without affecting the desktop view.
